description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing at the ends. The body is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each displaying a natural wood grain texture and subtle carved lines. Two prominent dark metal hoops encircle the barrel, reinforcing its structure and adding a rugged, utilitarian appearance. One end of the barrel features a metal spigot or tap, suggesting it is designed to dispense liquids, while the opposite end is plain. The wood appears well-finished but matte, and the metal bands have a slightly worn, matte finish, indicating durability and frequent use. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as taverns, cellars, or outdoor markets.
description: A wooden barrel with dark metal hoops and a spigot, featuring a natural wood grain and a sturdy, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from the end with a visible metal spigot protruding from the center, surrounded by wood grain and encircled by a metal hoop.
description_view_1: A side view showing the full length of the barrel, highlighting the two dark metal hoops and the wood's natural grain pattern.
description_view_2: The opposite end of the barrel, plain and without a spigot, with visible wood grain and a surrounding metal hoop.
description_view_3: Another side view similar to view 1, emphasizing the curvature of the barrel and the placement of the metal hoops.
